commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an Actions cache for the Linux `build-linux-cli` swiftly home/bootstrap, centralizes `SWIFT_VERSION`, and skips the bootstrap download when the cache restores the expected files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR changes CI setup performance rather than fixing a reproducible application bug.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining decision is maintainer review of the CI cache boundary and contributor proof, not an automated code repair.

target.title: ci: cache Linux Swift toolchains
target.body:
## Summary
- cache the Linux swiftly home/bootstrap used by the `build-linux-cli` matrix
- centralize the Swift version in workflow `env.SWIFT_VERSION`
- skip the swiftly bootstrap download/init when the cache restores both `swiftly` and `env.sh`

## Why
Every PR runs the Linux x64 and arm64 CLI jobs, and each job currently downloads and initializes swiftly before installing Swift 6.2.1. Caching `~/.local/share/swiftly` plus the `~/.local/bin/swiftly` bootstrap lets warm runs reuse the toolchain/bootstrap while keeping the existing `swiftly install ... --use` command as the correctness gate.

This does not change test coverage or branch-protection behavior. Cold runs still follow the existing download/init/install path; warm runs avoid the redundant bootstrap download and should reduce Linux job setup time for contributors.

## Validation
- workflow YAML parsed with `yaml.safe_load`
- checked the `build-linux-cli` job contains `actions/cache@v4` with an OS/arch/Swift-version scoped key
- extracted the embedded Swift install shell step and ran `bash -n`
- simulated a cache-hit home locally with fake `swiftly`/`swift`; the step printed `Using cached swiftly bootstrap.` and completed through `swift --version`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: yuxin-qiao
